name: Tokyo Metropolis (Japan)

colors.symbolism: Edo-murasaki, the traditional purple dye of old Edo
purity and the bright future of the metropolis

description: An Edo-purple field charged at the centre with a white emblem consisting of six pointed rays emanating from a circular hub, a stylised abstraction of the kanji 東 ('east').
history: The Tokyo Metropolitan symbol was originally adopted in November 1889 as the symbol of Tokyo City; with the formation of Tokyo Metropolis (Tokyo-to) in 1943 it was inherited by the new entity. The flag, with the white symbol on an Edo-purple field, was officially adopted on 1 October 1964. A separate, modern ginkgo-leaf metropolitan symbol (the so-called shocho) was adopted in 1989, but the 1964 flag remains the official metropolitan flag.

features.symbolism: Edo-murasaki (Edo purple), a dye colour historically associated with the city of Edo, the predecessor of modern Tokyo
a stylised representation of the kanji 東 ('east'), the first character of Tokyo (東京). Six rays radiating from a central point evoke the sun and the development of the metropolis in all directions.
